# Hydra X

 3 Reviews 4.5/5  ★ ★ ★ ★ ★

A manufacturing execution system from MPDV.

Write a Review

Hydra X: Shop Floor Overview

Hydra X: Customizable UI

Hydra X: Dashboard 

Hydra X: Quality Chart

Hydra X: Planning Start End

Hydra X: OEE KPI

Hydra X: Manufacturing Software

Hydra X: Terminal Line Status

Hydra X: AIP SWA

## Product Overview

HYDRA X, previously known as HYDRA MES, is an advanced Manufacturing Execution System (MES) designed by MPDV that goes beyond the traditional scope of MES by integrating support processes such as intralogistics and operator guidance in complex assembly processes. It’s made to continuously monitor, control, and optimize production, allowing for more efficient utilization of company resources. [...] The HYDRA X platform has been developed to integrate with leading ERP systems, including SAP, Microsoft, and Oracle.

## Pros

 Gathers real-time shop floor data
 Great customer support
 Logical, intuitive UI
 Seamless integrations with backend ERPs
 Scalable for growing and enterprise-level corporations

## Cons

 High initial cost
 Limited one-piece flow support
 Confusing UI for some users

## Target Market

Mid-sized manufacturers to large enterprises in the electronics, automotive, food and beverage, and medical devices industries.

results.title: Quality Management with MES HYDRA X
results.content:
## Continuous improvement

In addition to processing external complaints - i.e. initiated by the customer - mApps from HYDRA X use recorded quality data including the failure cause to automatically trigger internal complaints. An optional Workflow Management then ensures that individually defined processes are adhered to and that the user knows at all times to what extend the complaint has been processed or which action is next. The Measure Management guarantees that causes are eliminated in the long term - entirely in line with the continuous improvement process (CIP).

## Failure prevention [...] ## Download further information on quality management

## Our HYDRA X categories at a glance

Order Management

Keep an eye on your orders at all times

Resource Management Icon

Making optimum use of your resources

material management icon

Your material always on the move

assembly management icon

Complex processes permanently transparent

quality management icon

Your quality always assured

HR Management icon

Your workforce is always ready for action

information management, icon

Your information always available

AI Suite

AI Suite – Artificial Intelligence out of the box

### Would you like more information? We are happy to help.

Just fill in the form below. We will take care of your inquiry promptly. [...] ## Less scrap and lower costs

HYDRA X supports you in inspection planning, operator self-testing and determining the usage decision. In addition, flexible evaluations of the inspection results help to identify trends and errors in the processes at an early stage, and to determine their causes. Measures to eliminate defects can then be defined and their effectiveness monitored. This leads to the reduction of scrap and therefore costs. With HYDRA X, you see quality assurance as an integrated process that runs in parallel with the production management and thus avoiding redundant data storage in several systems. The system provides a complete view - from goods receipt along the entire manufacturing process to goods issue.

## Optimized goods receipt inspection

results.title: How I would fix Hydra in a couple easy steps. - Reddit
results.content:
Unless I am missing something I think these 4 changes would go a long way in curbing the frustration. Do you think these changes would be enough? Are these the biggest problems? What did I miss, and how dumb are my ideas???

TLDR: IMO these are the 4 main problems and easy fixes:

1.   Serpants will - make so that only one head can have it up at a time.

2.   Trunda - fix a2

3.   Hydra Progression - lower chest point thresholds by 30-50% to match decrease in damage.

4.   Decapitated HP pool - do not respawn heads when depleted, reduce damage instead.

results.title: Nixos-24.11-small (hydra/cache) broken? - Help - NixOS Discourse
results.content:
# Nixos-24.11-small (hydra/cache) broken?

Hi,  
(Sorry, didn’t find any better place/category to post this.)  
I am using the channel “nixos-24.11-small” and starting today (2024-04-15) or maybe yesterday (2024-04-14) any `nixos-rebuild` or `nix-shell` appears to build every package from source (vs. downloading it from cache.nixos.org).

`nixos-rebuild`
`nix-shell`

Also `hydra-check` does not seem to find any successful package build in this channel:

`hydra-check`

$ hydra-check --channel nixos/release-24.11-small bash  
Build Status for nixpkgs.bash.x86\_64-linux on nixos/release-24.11-small  
:warning: This job is not a member of the latest evaluation of its jobset. This means it was removed or had an evaluation error.

:warning: [...] Sorry, I am still not convinced. :slight_smile:

:slight_smile:

If you’re missing the cache, it means one of two things:

Either way it’s a result of your config, which you haven’t shared, so best we can do is guess likely causes.

I believe I am within this “defined set of packages” as hardly any local compiling has occurred before.  
I do not see why this defined set should change seemingly randomly. [...] :warning:

Yet, I did not find any (obvious) indication of a major issue anywhere.

Switching to the “nixos-24.11” channel fixes the issue.

-small channels are only meant for use in cases where you use some core group of packages that hydra has built before that channel advances. AIUI hydra builds all packages only on the regular channels. So this is a sign that -small is not the channel you want.

But the list of packages I am using has not changed.  
(Virtually) all packages I am using were pulled from cache.nixos.org before.

[…] some core group of packages that hydra has built before that channel advances.
